prompt:
We keep tripping over urllib.parse in our link-rewriting service — parse_qsl silently drops things, query order gets mangled through dict round-trips, and half the module's behavior toggles on keyword flags nobody remembers. I want a small URL toolkit, urlkit.py, written from scratch. Hard rule: no urllib anywhere in it (the test suite literally checks sys.modules).

parse(s) -> URL object. Accepts absolute URLs `scheme://host[:port][/path][?query][#fragment]` and relative ones (just path/query/fragment, scheme and host are then None). Scheme and host are case-insensitive so lowercase them at parse time; path/query/fragment keep their case. Port becomes an int; a non-numeric port, an empty one after the colon, or anything outside 0-65535 is a ValueError. We never use userinfo, so an @ in the authority should be rejected loudly rather than half-parsed. Missing port/fragment are None, missing query is []. A trailing bare '?' just means empty query; a bare '#' means fragment "" (and it comes back in str()).

The query is a LIST of (key, value) string tuples — order and duplicates preserved, that's the whole point. Both keys and values are percent-decoded (UTF-8) with '+' meaning space; a broken escape like %GZ or a truncated %2 is a ValueError. Pairs without '=' get value "". Empty pair segments (a=1&&b=2) are skipped. Methods: get(key) first value or None, get_all(key), add(key, value) appends, remove(key) removes all occurrences and returns how many, replace(key, value) swaps the FIRST occurrence in place (same position!) and drops the rest, appending instead if the key wasn't there.

str(url) rebuilds it: query values re-encoded (space as '+', unreserved chars A-Za-z0-9._~- bare, everything else %XX uppercase-hex UTF-8), a pair with empty value rendered as the bare key.

Also normalize_path(path) as a module function — resolve '.' and '..' segments the RFC way: '..' pops, clamped at the root for absolute paths but PRESERVED when a relative path climbs out ('a/b/../../../c' is '../c'), trailing '/.' and '/..' keep the trailing slash ('/a/b/..' is '/a/'), and doubled slashes are NOT collapsed — that changes meaning on some backends. url.normalize() applies it to the path and returns self.

Acceptance = test_urlkit.py, run python3 test_urlkit.py, leave the file alone. Stdlib only (minus urllib, obviously).
